title: add claude support
business_req: Integrate Claude to enhance automation and efficiency of LinkedIn skills, providing users with more powerful tools for job applications and networking.
user_story: As a user, I want to leverage Claude's advanced capabilities to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of LinkedIn automation tasks, such as applying for jobs and reaching out to contacts, so that I can maximize my professional networking opportunities.
acceptance_criteria: Claude integration is successfully added to the existing LinkedIn skills.
All existing skills (Job Auto-Apply, Profile Scraper, Contact Reacher) can utilize Claude's capabilities without errors.
New tests are written and existing tests are updated to cover Claude integration.
Documentation is updated to reflect the integration of Claude, including usage examples and any new commands.
Performance benchmarks show no significant degradation in task execution times after Claude integration.
technical_notes: Ensure that Claude's API is compatible with the existing Playwright MCP tools. Consider potential rate limits and authentication requirements when integrating Claude. Review the existing agents to determine if any need to be modified to support Claude's capabilities. Pay special attention to the Automation Agent and Web Structure Agent for potential updates.

title: add UI supporrt
business_req: Enhance user engagement by providing a graphical interface for LinkedIn automation tasks, making it accessible to non-technical users.
user_story: As a user, I want a user-friendly interface to easily access and manage LinkedIn automation tasks, so that I can efficiently utilize the automation tools without needing to interact with the command line.
acceptance_criteria: UI components are integrated with existing Playwright MCP tools.
UI allows users to select and invoke each of the three skills: Job Auto-Apply, Profile Scraper, and Contact Reacher.
UI displays real-time feedback and status updates for each skill execution.
UI adheres to LinkedIn's terms of service and does not trigger anti-detection mechanisms.
technical_notes: Ensure that the UI components are responsive and compatible with various browsers. Consider using a frontend framework like React or Vue.js for dynamic UI updates. Pay special attention to LinkedIn's DOM structure and potential changes that might affect the UI's interaction with the underlying automation scripts.

title: add K8S support
business_req: Enable scalable and efficient deployment of LinkedIn Skills services using Kubernetes on AWS infrastructure.
user_story: As an engineer, I want to deploy LinkedIn Skills services on Kubernetes to leverage its scalability and manageability features, ensuring high availability and efficient resource utilization.
acceptance_criteria: Kubernetes (K8S) manifests are created for all services in the repository.
AWS EKS cluster is configured and services are deployable to it.
CI/CD pipeline is updated to support deployment to K8S.
Documentation is updated to include K8S deployment instructions.
technical_notes: Ensure that the Kubernetes manifests include necessary configurations such as resource limits, environment variables, and secrets management. Consider using Helm charts for easier management of K8S resources. Pay attention to AWS IAM roles and permissions required for EKS. Update the CI/CD pipeline to include steps for building Docker images and pushing them to a container registry before deploying to K8S.

title: add csv output
business_req: Enable users to export ticket data in CSV format for easier data analysis and reporting.
user_story: As a project manager, I want to export ticket data as a CSV file so that I can analyze and share project progress with stakeholders.
acceptance_criteria: The system should generate a CSV file containing ticket data when requested.
The CSV output should include all ticket fields: ID, title, description, priority, business requirements, stakeholder, and user story.
The CSV file should be downloadable from the UI.
Ensure the CSV export functionality is covered by unit tests.
technical_notes: Consider using Python's built-in csv module to handle CSV generation. Ensure that the CSV export functionality does not block the main application thread. You may need to modify the UI to add a button for downloading the CSV file. Ensure that the CSV generation handles special characters and large datasets efficiently.

title: Implement Ticket Auto-Creation Feature
description: Develop the auto-creation feature using the creator agent to generate structured ticket drafts from a given repository.
business_req: Automate the ticket creation process to improve efficiency and reduce manual workload.
user_story: As a developer, I want the system to automatically create tickets from a repository so that I can focus on resolving issues rather than documenting them.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

title: Enhance Ticket Enrichment Process
description: Improve the ticket enrichment process by refining the enricher agent to provide more detailed and useful ticket information.
business_req: Provide enriched ticket details to assist developers in understanding and resolving issues more effectively.
user_story: As a product manager, I want enriched ticket details so that I can prioritize and assign tasks more effectively.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks specific acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

title: Optimize Repo Context Fetching
description: Optimize the fetch_context function to handle larger repositories efficiently without exceeding the 50 KB limit.
business_req: Ensure the system can handle larger repositories without performance degradation.
user_story: As a developer, I want the system to efficiently fetch context from large repositories so that I can work with comprehensive data.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks specific acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

title: Integrate OpenAI API Key Configuration
description: Allow users to configure their OpenAI API key securely through the environment settings.
business_req: Provide a secure method for users to configure their OpenAI API key, ensuring that sensitive information is not exposed.
user_story: As a developer, I want to securely configure my OpenAI API key so that I can use the AI features without exposing sensitive information.
acceptance_criteria: Users can set their OpenAI API key in the .env file without exposing it in the codebase.
The application reads the OpenAI API key from the environment variables at runtime.
If the OpenAI API key is missing or invalid, the application should log an appropriate error message and disable AI features.
Ensure that the OpenAI API key is not logged or exposed in any application logs.
technical_notes: Ensure that the OpenAI API key is accessed using os.environ to maintain security. Consider using a library like python-dotenv to load environment variables from a .env file. Validate the presence of the API key at application startup and handle errors gracefully if the key is missing or invalid.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references, making it incomplete and untestable.

title: Implement Unit Tests for TicketStore
description: Develop comprehensive unit tests for the TicketStore to ensure reliable ticket storage and retrieval operations.
business_req: Ensure the reliability and correctness of ticket storage operations, as these are critical for maintaining accurate and accessible ticket data.
user_story: As a QA engineer, I want comprehensive unit tests for the TicketStore so that I can ensure its reliability and correctness, preventing data loss or corruption.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover all public methods of the TicketStore class.
Tests verify both successful and failure scenarios for ticket storage and retrieval.
Tests ensure that the in-memory dictionary and the JSON file are consistent after operations.
Tests are written using pytest and achieve at least 90% code coverage for TicketStore.
All tests pass without errors when running the full test suite.
technical_notes: The TicketStore uses an in-memory dictionary and writes to 'data/tickets.json'. Ensure tests mock file I/O operations to avoid actual file writes during testing. Consider edge cases such as empty storage, duplicate ticket IDs, and concurrent modifications.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references, making it incomplete.

title: Improve UI for Ticket Management
description: Enhance the user interface to provide a more intuitive and user-friendly experience for managing tickets.
business_req: Enhance the user interface to provide a more intuitive and user-friendly experience for managing tickets, thereby improving user satisfaction and efficiency.
user_story: As a user, I want an intuitive UI for managing tickets so that I can efficiently handle my tasks without confusion or unnecessary steps.
acceptance_criteria: The UI should display a list of tickets with clear visual hierarchy and intuitive navigation.
Users should be able to filter and sort tickets based on status, priority, and date.
The ticket detail view should be accessible with a single click and display all relevant information.
The UI should be responsive and function correctly on both desktop and mobile devices.
All UI changes should pass existing frontend tests and any new tests created for additional functionality.
technical_notes: Consider using a modern frontend framework like React or Vue.js to enhance the UI components. Ensure that the UI changes do not affect the existing API endpoints. Pay attention to the in-memory data structure in `app/storage.py` to ensure UI updates reflect real-time data changes.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks specific acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

user_story: As a developer, I want detailed API documentation so that I can integrate and use the system effectively.
validation_notes: The ticket lacks acceptance criteria, related files, technical notes, and change references.

description: Develop robust error handling mechanisms for API calls to ensure graceful failure and informative error messages.
business_req: Ensure the system handles API errors gracefully to improve reliability and user experience.
user_story: As a developer, I want robust error handling for API calls so that I can quickly diagnose and resolve issues.
